releases.name: Proton proton_10.0 (WOW64 with Vulkan & GStreamer for Termux - xuser)
releases.body:
# Proton proton_10.0 - WOW64 with Vulkan & GStreamer for Termux (xuser)

## 版本信息
- **Proton 分支**: proton_10.0
- **架构**: True WOW64 (同时支持 32 位和 64 位 Windows 应用程序)
- **平台**: Termux (Android)
- **图形 API**: Vulkan 支持已启用
- **多媒体**: 完整 GStreamer 支持
- **用户名**: xuser (已移除 Steam 优化)

## 特性
✓ 真正的 WOW64 架构
✓ 同时支持 32 位和 64 位 Windows 应用程序
✓ Vulkan 图形 API 支持
✓ 完整的 GStreamer 支持
✓ 中文环境支持
✓ 基于 Valve Proton，但移除了 Steam 特定优化
✓ 默认用户名改为 xuser

## 重要变更
- 所有对 `steamuser` 的引用已改为 `xuser`
- 移除了 Steam 特定的优化和组件
- 更适合通用 Windows 应用程序运行

## 安装说明
解压后，将 `proton-proton-wine-10.0-4-b8fdff8e1f8-wow64-xuser` 文件夹放置在 Termux 的合适位置，并设置环境变量。

## 构建信息
- 构建时间: 
- 提交: c139ecff7e39b1a0e194826fccf83496e3dbbfa9
- 工作流: Wine and Proton 无bootBuilder

releases.name: 7.1.3glibc-Amod
releases.body:
原版更新重要内容，以及Amod内容:
~~~~增加了MIDI支持，允许部分游戏播放 MIDI 音频
~~~~改进了游戏控制器支持，您现在可以选择性地使用XInput或DInput映射。线性触发器现在适用于两种 
       映射模式。对于触发器按钮，如果它无法正常工作，请尝试调整触发器按钮模式以Settings匹配您的控 
      制器。
~~~~增加了自动设置容器Locale的功能，现在您也可以LC_ALL在容器编辑界面中调整设置。
~~~~增加了横屏和竖屏模式切换功能，现在您可以使用容器的分辨率设置或Toggle Orientation左侧导航栏 
       的按钮切换到竖屏模式。
~~~~改进了OpenXR支持。非常感谢Luboš V.
~~~~修复了导致启动时崩溃的问题。
~~~~修复 3DoF 支持以确保其正常运行，而不是在 2DoF 模式下运行。
~~~~修复了 Meta Quest 3 的一些图形故障。
~~~~在设置中添加了控制器映射功能。
~~~~修复了QuestOS V69上的黑屏问题。
~~~~添加了 gst-libav。

Amod更改内容:
~~~~默认wine9.2，以及wine9.16
~~~~关于gst解码。9.2比较完善，9.16目前没有画面只有声音！
~~~~添加dosbox模拟器！使用方法大概是盘符(比如e:)回车，cd 游戏文件夹，play回车！
~~~~添加更多小游戏和补丁
~~~~添加更多dxvk和驱动，box64等
~~~~添加media补丁和注册表听说可以解决生化危机8
~~~~添加部分变量，比如tu_allow_oob_indirect_ubo_loads和PULSE_LATENCY_MSEC
~~~~添加box64maxcpu，分为0-4-8-16-32
~~~~更改部分默认组件和其他设置
~~~~提供sw选项，
~~~~提供tu-debug另一个指令nofsdt，部分游戏会有提速！
~~~~添加更多虚拟按键皮肤图标！
~~~~其他部分更改！

releases.name: GameHub Lite v5.3.3 RC-1 [PRE-RELEASE]
releases.body:
GameHub Lite v5.3.3 RC-1 [PRE-RELEASE]

This is an experimental build that adds **Micro SD Card Support** for **Steam Games** based on GameHub v5.3.3.

There is no support for this build, this is meant for those who are helping out or want to tinker.

**IMPORTANT:**

- Enable SD Card support in the settings before downloading Steam games
- Switching storage location back and forth hasn’t been tested yet.
- It will create a directory named `GHL` on your SD Card where the downloaded Steam games will be stored. (FF modders, go wild).
- This version can be installed alongside GHL v5.1.3 for easy testing.
- The full Steam client doesn’t work in this version, use Steam Lightweight or use GameHub Lite 5.1.3 which is stable.

releases.name: GameHub Lite v5.1.0 Patch
releases.body:
# Patch & Patch Generation (GameHub v5.1.0)

The files needed to contribute to the project and patch GameHub 5.1.0 yourself are now released. 

- Completely reworked the initial patching approach.
- *Revanced* patch is a work in progress, feel free to contribute to this if you are comfortable with revanced. 
- Easy update patches after modifications 
- Work on macOS/Linux. (maybe windows?)

Read the [README](https://github.com/Producdevity/gamehub-lite/blob/master/README.md) file for a full explanation.

### FAQ

- Does this work with GameHub version *not 5.1.0*
- **no.**


--- 


## How It Works

The patcher uses a multi-step process:

1. **Decompile** - Uses apktool to decompile the original APK to smali bytecode
2. **Delete** - Removes telemetry SDKs, unused assets, and tracking libraries
3. **Patch** - Applies unified diff patches to modify smali code
4. **Add** - Copies new files (resources, additional smali)
5. **Rebuild** - Reassembles the APK using apktool
6. **Sign** - Signs with a debug keystore for installation

## Patch Contents

| Category | Count | Description |
|----------|-------|-------------|
| Deletions | 3,385 | Telemetry SDKs, tracking code, unused assets |
| Additions | 2,856 | New resources, modified assets, new features |
| Modifications | 223 | Smali code patches for behavior changes |

### Removed Components

- **Native libraries**: libumeng-spy.so, libcrashsdk.so, libalicomphonenumberauthsdk_core.so, etc.
- **SDKs**: Umeng Analytics, JPush, Firebase Analytics, Tencent login
- **Assets**: Splash video, auth videos, emoji font (saves ~30MB)
- **Permissions**: Location, contacts, phone state, ad tracking

### Added Features

- Custom splash/intro video from community CDN
- Local game ID copy functionality
- Offline mode improvements
- News page integration with community API

## For Developers

### Regenerating Patches

If you've modified the Lite APK and want to update the patches:

```bash
./generate-patches.sh [path/to/original.apk] [path/to/lite.apk]
```

This will:
1. Decompile both APKs
2. Generate diff patches for modified files
3. Copy new files to patches directory
4. Create deletion and addition lists

### Patch Directory Structure

```
patches/
├── files_to_delete.txt        # List of files to remove
├── files_to_add.txt             # List of files to add
├── files_to_patch.txt          # List of files to modify
├── diffs/                                # Unified diff patches
│   ├── AndroidManifest.xml.patch
│   ├── smali/...
│   └── res/...
├── new_files/                        # New files to add
│   ├── res/...
│   └── smali_classes10/...     # Robot language files to add
└── stats.txt                           # Patch statistics (ignored and generated on patch-generation)
```

releases.name: GameHub Lite v5.1.4
releases.body: GameHub Lite adds **RTS Touch Controls**. Massive thanks to @Nightwalker743 for their contributions! **Changelogs** - Add RTS Touch Controls - @Nightwalker743 - Improve stability of `patch.sh` script - @sgumil - add APK build GitHub workflow - @ObfuscatedVoid **RTS Controls feature list:** **_Tap to Click_** - Tap anywhere to move cursor and click (Like move cursor to touchpoint on Winlator) **_Drag for Box Selection_** - Drag finger to create selection box (left mouse button held during drag) **_Long Press for Right-Click_** - Hold finger for 300ms to trigger right-click **_Double-Tap for Double-Click_** - Tap twice within 250ms and 50 pixels to double-click **_Two-Finger Pan for Camera_** - Drag with two fingers to pan camera **_Pinch-to-Zoom_*** - Pinch two fingers inward to zoom out, pinch outward to zoom in (mouse wheel scroll) **_Toggle Switch_*** - Enable/disable RTS controls in Settings > Controls tab (in the side overlay menu when you're in a game). _The two gestures that have an asterisk can be customized in the RTS Gesture Settings menu._

releases.name: GameHub Lite v5.1.3
releases.body: We recommend using [Obtanium](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=7HP67K5Q6Fo) to keep your GameHub Lite up-to-date. Thank to The412Banner for making the video tutorial! Please check [EmuReady](https://www.emuready.com) for recommended settings if a game doesn't run well before asking in the [Discord](https://discord.gg/CYhCzApXav) server for help.

(Alt Antutu is a workaround for users that want to use Antutu and faced issues with the Beacon launcher)

Thanks The412Banner for helping out!

releases.name: GameHub Lite v5.1.1
releases.body: changelog: - Uses GameHub Lite v4 as it's basis instead of v3 - Resolved broken intents with launchers (ESDE/Beacon/Dajisho) - Fixes brightness bug - Adds EmuReady compatibility badge back - Uses the new EmuReady workers server for better privacy - Removed the Notification Permissions popup properly - Removed the Free Games section since it doesn't work anymore - Removed the unneeded resources files for services we're not using anymore - Restored the Microphone Permission for games that require it Full credits go to [Trixarian](https://github.com/Trixarian), this is just a signed version to make it easy to update from `v5.1.0` --- Please check [EmuReady](https://www.emuready.com) for recommended settings if a game doesn't run well before asking in the [Discord](https://discord.gg/CYhCzApXav) server for help.

releases.name: GameHub Lite v5.1.0
releases.body:
A restoration build of GameHub Lite V4 before everything fell apart. I will publish the code for the workers and a patch very soon, for now enjoy a working GameHub Lite app again.

We'll focus on restoring the previous functionality first, before fixing and adding functionality. So please only report issues that were not present in GameHub Lite V4 or in the official GameHub app.

---

### Explanation for the different versions

You’ll often see an emulator distribute different versions with a name suffixed like **PUBG**, **Antutu**, **Optimized**, **Genshin**, **Ludashi** and other names. Here follows an explanation for what is what, is still has slight differences between manufacturers and even different packagenames but it should give you a rough understanding.

—-

*The different versions are identical. What we do is a very old android trick to gain extra performance on some devices.*

**Antutu**

Some manufacturers “cheat” by setting the governor to performance when they detect the Antutu package name. 

*Nerd explanation:*

The CPU governor essentially controls the CPU's frequency scaling. allowing it to operate at different clock speeds and voltages based on the system load. So making the CPU go fast for sustained usage, what is actually made for peak usage. This comes with a risk of overheating, but I don’t believe in this. Android does a well enough job of thermal management and makes it extremely hard for software to exceed what the hardware is capable of and damaging itself.


That said, it’s still extra heat. More heat == more bad. I just think it’s negligible, especially if your device has a fan.


**PUBG**

On a high level it’s the same as Antutu, but some slight differences that only benefit games. Think of network prioritization and touch input latency improvements. The manufacturers goal when they detect Antutu is **ALL THE POWER**. Benchmarks are relatively short and it makes them look better on comparison websites.


The goal for PUBG is more like **MORE POWER**, since the intention is often to have a game running for longer it has less aggressive changes.


**TLDR and summary:**

**Antutu** spoofing:

    •    Maximum CPU/GPU frequencies unlocked  
    •    Aggressive performance governors  
    •    Short-duration performance boost (benchmark workload)  
    •    Thermal limits are less strict   
    •    All cores available  


**PUBG** and other games spoofing: 

    •    Sustained gaming performance profiles  
    •    GPU driver optimizations (Adreno/Mali game-specific paths)  
    •    Frame pacing and scheduling improvements  
    •    Reduced touch latency   
    •    Network QoS prioritization  
    •    Different thermal management (sustained vs burst)  
    •    Qualcomm “Game Performance Mode”      
    •    Sometimes enables features like frame-gen


**Ludashi** spoofing:

    •    Similar to Antutu but slightly less aggressive    
    •    Longer sustained performance boost (multi-minute tests)   
    •    Memory frequency optimization

releases.name: GameHub Lite v5.0.0
releases.body:
This is a temporary release that restores most of the functionality previously available in GameHub Lite v4.

GameHub Lite v4 stopped working after the former developer took down the Cloudflare Workers it relied on, citing privacy concerns. 

We are currently developing a community-funded solution to provide a more stable and permanent setup.

In the meantime, this version includes most of the core features but may display some visual inconsistencies and lacks certain extras that were present in v4, such as the news and free games sections.

Special thanks to **The412Banner** and **Trixarian**!

[Join the EmuReady Discord for support and more information](https://discord.gg/CYhCzApXav)

🚀 **XoDos** Emulator (arm64) - Major Update!

Welcome to a huge update for XoDos! This release packs a massive punch with refinements across the entire app, from a polished UI to powerful new under-the-hood features for both Wine and the Proot environment. Get ready for a smoother, more compatible, and more controllable experience.

✨** What's New in the App?**

· 🎨 Updated & Fixed App UI: A refreshed and more stable user interface for a better overall experience.
· ⚙️ New Quick-Settings Menu: Added new settings for instant access to install DXVK/D3D and drivers for Wine anywhere, anytime! No more complicated commands.
· 🚀 Termux-X11 Performance Boost: Updated to the latest official code! Now featuring hardware buffer support for significantly improved performance and smoothness.
· 📋 Restored Start Menu & Widgets: The convenient start menu is back! Plus, we've added handy Termux widgets for quicker access.
· 🍷 Wine Bionic Updated: Upgraded to Wine 10.0-rc3 x86_64 for much better application and game compatibility.
· 🔧 Fixed Desktop Scripts: Fixed scripts for both Wine Glibc and Bionic. You can now properly stop the desktop from the settings with the "Stop Desktop" button.
· 🖥️ Fixed Native Desktop Launcher: Resolved a bug in the hardware acceleration mode selector that was incorrectly choosing VIRGL mode for Linux apps.
· 🐛 Fixed Proot Desktop Script: The script for launching the Proot desktop has been fixed and stabilized.
· 🖱️ Fixed External Mouse Support: Improved the mouse capturing method for a more reliable external mouse experience.

---

🪄 What's New in the Proot System?

The integrated Proot environment has received a massive upgrade, turning it into a powerhouse for Windows applications.

· 🍷 Wine 9.17: Added the latest and greatest Wine version (9.17) for cutting-edge support.
· 🤖 New Box64 Integration: Added brand-new Box64 for vastly improved x86_64 Windows game and application support on ARM64 devices!
· 🎯 Experimental Turnip Drivers: Added new Turnip drivers (Mesa RadV). They can be installed via .bat files for now. Once stable, they will be integrated into the quick-settings menu just like Bionic and Glibc.
· ⚡ DXVK & Wined3D Installation: Added the ability to install DXVK and Wined3D directly from .bat files inside the Proot environment, same as the drivers.
· 📊 MS Office Installation Fixed: MS Office can now be installed and fixed anytime directly from the desktop.
· 🎮 Updated OpenGL/Zink: Updated and fixed OpenGL and Zink implementations for better graphics rendering.
· 🔬 Beta VIRGL Drivers: Added VIRGL drivers for both Wine and Linux applications (marked as beta for now).
· ...and more! Various under-the-hood tweaks and fixes for a rock-solid experience.

   Nostalgia meets modern! Choose from **Windows XP**, **7**, or **11** themes for your XoDos desktop.  

3. **📚 Beginner-Friendly Help Tutorial**  
   New interactive guide to help newcomers dive into XoDos with ease.  

4. **⚙️ Quick Settings for New Users**  
   Simplified settings panel to configure essentials in seconds.  

5. **👥 Dual User Mode for Proot/Kali**  
   Choose between **Root User** or **Normal User** for Kali Desktop and Proot environments.  

6. **🚀 Auto Hardware Acceleration**  
   XoDos Desktop now automatically optimizes performance based on your hardware.  

7. **🐧 Multi-Distro Support**  
   Install and boot **multiple Linux distros** in one environment. Freedom to choose!  

8. **📊 Progress Bar for Install/Restore**  
   Track real-time progress during installations or system restores.  

9. **🛠️ Enhanced Bionic Drivers**  
   Improved DX11 game compatibility (DX12 still experimental).  

10. **🔄 Legacy Glibc Support**  
    Added backward compatibility for devices requiring older drivers.  

11. **🌐 French Language Support**  
    Fully localized UI and documentation for French-speaking users!  

---

## 🛠️ **Fixed & Improved**  
1. **🐛 Installation/Restore Bug Fix**  
   Resolved issues causing failed installations or restores.  

2. **🧹 Storage Cleaner for Wine/Linux**  
   Free up space effortlessly with the new cleanup helper tool.  

3. **✅ Pre-Launch System Checks**  
   XoDos now verifies installed systems before launching Wine/Linux.  

---

## ⚙️ **Technical Enhancements**  
1. **⚡ Updated DXVK Files**  
   Latest DXVK versions for both **Bionic** and **Glibc** environments.  

2. **🎮 New 3D Performance Tests**  
   Replaced outdated Winlator tests with advanced DX11/DX12 diagnostics.  

3. **🔧 Script Optimizations**  
   Smoother execution and stability fixes across Wine/Linux subsystems.  

4. **🧪 Hardware Validation Tools**  
   Added diagnostics to troubleshoot compatibility issues.  

---

**...and more!**  
Smaller tweaks, UI polish, and under-the-hood optimizations for a seamless experience.  

---

**⚠️ Report issues [here](https://github.com/xodiosx/XoDos)**  
**❤️ Thank you for your support!**

releases.name: ludashi 2.9 (Beta)
releases.body:
What's new:

 From Pypetto-Crypto 

⚙️ forcefully disable compute BCn on Adreno devices
📦 assets: add dxvk sarek for Mali GPUs
💻 MainActivity: create Winlator folder before proper app initialization
🖥️ XServerDisplayActivity: remove Zink DLLs for arm64ec, avoid faking device and vendor id when dxvk-sarek is selected, force activity to always pick the highest available refresh rate
📫 ContentsManager: add Proton content profile
 
From StevenMX

🛠️ New winlator fps hud, with GPU/CPU/Wats/Temp
⚒️ New universal FPS Limiter for any game 

🖥️  sidebar appearance has been changed, adding Super Resolution, DLS, HDR, Natural, and CRT filters (working in-game)

🪧 changed the Visual of the Game Shortcuts (Some images may not be loaded correctly, but you can change the icon)

🖥️ New Direct Rendering Mode, for better latency and consequently better performance
how it works:
Direct Rendering mode in winlator, modify the dri3 path to do a direct scanout with AHardwarebuffer ogl images to the screen,skipping the windows rendering,this can reduce the CPU usage and improve latency, this option is only available in shortcuts, filters/magnifier/fps limiter will not work with it, and will only work in games in Fullscreen mode, it's still experimental. 

🏷️ changed the redmagic build package for PUBG, since it seems to be a better option (for more phones) than the previous one (genshin impact)

📌 this update needs reinstallation of course

releases.name: Ludashi 2.8.2
releases.body:
What's new:

🛠️ devices that weren't working should now work. thanks to @kostasgangstar 

📦 Fixed a regression in box64 in 32bits games

💾 Assets: update wrapper, turnip, and box64/fexcore to the latest versions 

🎮 Controller: fixed Wine 9.20, and some others issues, if input don't work, try disabling "Exclusive"

💻 Added support for compute shaders BCn decompression as an option, improving performance for Mali Devices

🖥️ GraphicsDriverConfigDialog: add option to change/spoof GPU name, device and vendor id

⚙️ Container: add internal storage as a container drive
  
📌 reinstall from scratch before update

releases.name: Ludashi 2.8
releases.body:
What's new:
some Quality of Life (QoL) improvements 

🎮 New Controller Implementation 
The new implementation of controller brings varying compatibility with Xinput/Dinput with most games (like the one in cmod v13, but Reworked), it also brings Multiplayer support since you can connect more than one controller, allows custom package names,and without overhead. thanks to @Vivsi1 and @Pipetto-crypto

🗂️ Added a File Manager
the new file manager allows you to create a shorcut for the game without need to enter the container, Please note that some games may still require you to install content such as vcredist, check [dependencies](https://github.com/K11MCH1/Winlator101/releases/tag/deps) 

⚙️ Added a Driver Download Manager 
Like some other emulators, you can now download drivers directly from the app, and you can also edit the default repositories to your preference. 

🖼️ added support for custom Icons
Just click on the game icon and then you can change.

PS: this update needs full reinstall, otherwise nothing will work

releases.name: ludashi 2.7.4
releases.body:
what's new:
💾 add a few more entries to device memory, 8192 MB,12288 MB,16384 MB

⚙️EnvVarsView: add one new TU_DEBUG option (deck_emu), see [Turnip_Deck](https://github.com/StevenMXZ/freedreno_turnip-CI/releases/tag/26.0.0)

🛠️ added IR3_SHADER_DEBUG and FD_DEV_FEATURES in environment variables, for greater convenience for those who need it.

🖥️ added support for [Config_Files](https://gist.github.com/bylaws/04130932e2634d1c6a2a9729e3940d60), (qgl_config.txt) for Qualcomm drivers, without Root, the default path for it is [storage/emulated/0/Winlator], you can use rendermode=0, for force sysmem on Qualcomm driver (like V819), improving performance by +10% on some games. 

Rendermode values:
0 - sysmem without binning
1 - gmem with binning
2 - gmem without binning
3 - sysmem with binning

releases.name: Ludashi 2.7.3
releases.body:
fixed some bugs basically 

what's new:
 🖥️ XServerDisplayActivity: remove seemingly unused function 
🛠️ Merge pull request from Arihany/winlator_bionic
 Fix prefix style package name collisions
 Fix DXVK semver compare with string equality bug fix
 Fix crash with prefixed vernames
💾 XServerDisplayActivity: set 
 WRAPPER_MAX_IMAGE_COUNT to 1 when immediate present mode is chosen

⚙️EnvVarsView: add two new TU_DEBUG options (forcecb) and (nocb), force concurrent Binning on Turnip, and force disable, some games are faster with CB, but some can be worse

releases.name: Ludashi 2.7.2
releases.body:
🔄 the wrapper has been updated. 
🖥️ improved performance for Mali and Adreno devices
🧹 improved BCn emulation Cache

releases.name: Ludashi 2.7.1
releases.body:
🛠️ Fixed long standing bug that caused A12 devices to crash when pressing Wrapper gear icon using Turnip driver

🖥️ Fixed regressions caused by the new BCn implementation

💾 Added new env vars for performance and stability

releases.name: Ludashi 2.7
releases.body:
🖥️ major update:

 - updated the wrapper
- Added BCn emulation for Mali GPUs
- Fixed long term bug that caused dxvk 2.x to not work on some Adreno devices.
- Partially fixed crash that happened on some A6xx when pressing GraphicsDriver gear icon
- improved overall performance
- Initial work to get HDR and CAS sharpening to work while game is running using vkBasalt ( in progress)
- issues tab on GitHub It's open again.

releases.name: Ludashi 2.6.2
releases.body:
🖥️ added new FEXCore Presets and Variables 
 💾 updated the contents Repo

releases.name: Ludashi 2.6.1
releases.body: 🛠️ Fixed compatibility with some Adreno GPUs

releases.name: Ludashi 2.6
releases.body:
# 📝 Changelogs

### 🎮 Performance

💾 Updated to the latest **Wrapper (v25)**, which promises to improve performance and compatibility across most GPUs.

📉 Added a new **"Blitting"** option. This rendering technique can provide a significant compatibility  increase in some applications. (For more information on how blitting works, see [here](https://matplotlib.org/stable/users/explain/animations/blitting.html)).

🎛️ Added new **Resource Type** options (dmabuf, opaque, ahb) in the Graphics Driver settings. These may affect compatibility, and their performance impact can vary depending on the specific device and game. (for Adreno GPUs, ahb is recommended)

🌋 Vulkan layers are now automatically extracted on boot.

### ⚙️ UI & QoL

🎉 StevenMXZ's Winlator-Contents repository now comes **pre-installed by default**..

### 📝 Notes
⚠️ full reinstallation is required 
🖥️ if your game is crashing, disable the box "disable KHR present wait"
🙏 Full development credit for the Bionic source goes to **Pipetto-crypto**.

releases.name: Ludashi 2.5
releases.body:

💾 updated the stuff from my repo of contents, go to Winlator Settings and Copy and paste - >

https://raw.githubusercontent.com/StevenMXZ/Winlator-Contents/main/contents.json

📝 all credits to Pipetto-Crypto

recommended to use my repo of contents, go to Winlator Settings and Copy and paste - >

# 📝 Changelogs

## 🖥️  Export/import Shortcut Presets

🛠️ Added option to export and import Box64Presets

🪡 Added option to import shortcuts

🔧  Major rework of Fexcore management

🔄 Replaced Frontend Export Path setting with a generic Winlator Path setting

✅ Allowed choosing custom shortcuts export path

## 🎮 Input 

🧹 Fixed crash when trying to show toast messages

🚮 Removed duplicate cursor speed setting

🛞 Moved gyro settings from SettingsFragment to InputControlsFragment

## 📺  XServer

💾 Returned fragment handling back into activity

## 📝 Notes

recommended to uninstall your old ludashi and install the new one again 

Full development credit goes to Pipetto-crypto

releases.name: Ludashi 2.3
releases.body:

## 📦 DXVKConfigDialog

Refactored setEnvVars handling for improved environment variable management

Moved ddrawrapper configuration into DXVKConfigDialog for better consistency


## 💾 WineD3DConfigDialog

Fixed minor typo in dialog strings

Added dedicated configuration dialog for wined3d to allow finer control

General bug fixes and internal refinements

all credits and development to Pipetto-Crypto

releases.name: Bionic Ludashi 2.2
releases.body:
# changelogs

⚙️ Fixed arm64ec containers

 💾 SettingsFragment: added an option to change downloadable contents URL,for every 
DXVK/box64/Fexcore asset, got to Winlator Settings and Copy - >
https://raw.githubusercontent.com/StevenMXZ/Winlator-Contents/main/contents.json 

📦 Updated fexcore and wowbox64 versions

🧹 Cleanup: removed unnecessary assets

⚙️ DXVKConfigDialog: blacklisted arm64ec DXVK on non-arm64ec containers

🖥️ GraphicsDriverConfigDialog: blacklisted unsupported drivers

🔧 Vulkan: slight refactoring

 Defaulted to turnip25.1.0 on supported devices

🔄 Replaced v762 and v805 with v819 (compatible with a6xx)

🛠 ContainerDetailFragment: partially fixed setupDXWrapper logic

🎛️ GuestProgramLauncherComponent: added required variables to disable Proton mediaconverter plugin

## 📝 Notes

Full development credit goes to Pipetto-crypto.

releases.name: Horizon V4 - Not Connected fix
releases.body: After minor changes like Box64, Wine (all have almost the same performance), DXVK and Turnip and me not having time to do anything with this project, it's finally getting a "Not Connected" fix related to the usage of /tmp directory on some devices by the termux-x11 library in the app. Should work on Android 15 with this fix as well, people already reported it working
IGNORE WINE HANGOVER PACKAGES ( NOT RELATED TO WINLATOR !! )

# Added

• Latest Box64 Is Added In Proot Version

• Added Cube D3D & SphereMap 3D Test 

• Added D3D9 Wrapper By ThirteenAG In Start Menu → Install →  WineD3D → Experimental 

• Added Core Fonts

• Added Additional x86 and X64 Visual C Packages

• Added Xna Framework 

• Added An Option To Backup And Import Registry And Saves Of Container 

• Many Other Things Are Also Added 

# New Game Presets 

• Fixed And Added Back Skyrim Game Preset 

• Added Virtua Tennis 3 & 4 Game Preset 

• Added Silent Hill Home Coming Game Preset 

# Changes

• Wine 9.2 Is Added And Made Default In Glibc 

• Game UX Is Disabled By Default ( This Fixes Virtua Tennis 4 And More ) 

• The Fixes Section Has Been Updated 

• Some Games Presets Are Updated 

• Many Other Things Are Changed And Updated 

# Note 

• I Decided To Only Upload Proot_Antutu And Glibc From Now

• Delete The Old Apk And Install The New One 

• Proot Is The Recommended Version 

• Glibc Apk Is Updated Again On Dec 1 2024 . Pls Update

Changes : 

•  Proot Builds Are Now Based On Winlator 8.0 Finally More Compatibility 

• 2.1 Is As Set As The Default GL VERSION

• Disable Vertex Array BGRA Is Disabled By Default 

• Default Gpu Model In Proot Builds Is Now Set To Nvdia GeForce 9800GT So Oblivion Launcher And More Can Detect And Give Settings 

• Box64 Version 0.2.8 From Winlator 7.1 Is Now The Default Version

Added :

• WineD3D 9.20 Added 

• BOX64_MMAP32 Environment Variable Added For Proot Builds

• More Things Are Added Which I Can't Remember Now Sorry 

Updated : 

• Updated The 3D Test ( Use This Instead Cube D3D Test It Is Removed Now )

• Updated VC Redist ( Improves Stability If Installed )

• Prince Of Persia 2008 And Prince Of Persia The Forgotten Sands Game Presets Are Added 

Removed : 

• Lot Of Unnecessary Things Are Removed 

Note : 

• To Fix Audio Issue On PES Games Just Turn Off Wine Esync In Container Settings 

• Skyrim Preset Is Now Removed From Proot Build Because Winlator 8.0 Doesn't Support To Disable Wow64 ( You Can Still Play Skyrim In WinlatorMali 1.8 And 1.7 )

• Delete The Old Apk And Install New One

• Overall Balanced Performance And Improved Compatibility On Proot Builds .

• Proot Is The Recommended Version 

• Glibc Version is Removed On 15 Nov 2024 Will Be Updated Later

releases.name: v0.9.0-prerelease
releases.body:
## What's Changed
* Updated Wrapper - initial PowerVR (Pixel 10) GPU support - thanks to @sharpy66 and @pipetto-crypto
* Added "use known config" and automatic best configs for Epic, GOG and Amazon by @unbelievableflavour in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/892
* Added Steam workshop implementation by @Nightwalker743 in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/977
* Added support for Steam game versions/branches by @morganwalkup in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/870
* Added "compatibility" filter by @unbelievableflavour in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/813
* Added downloads & storage manager by @xXJSONDeruloXx and @eredisg in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/878
* Added carousel layout by @xXJSONDeruloXx in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/797
* Fixes for launching from frontends by @jeremybernstein 
* Fix Steam cloud saves for games like Cult of the Lamb and Blue Revolver - apply UFS rootoverrides for Windows save paths by @kiequoo in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/839
* Screensaver for download screen OLED protection by @ObfuscatedVoid in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/1017
* fix for games with Steam Input by @kiequoo in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/1026
* Samsung DeX support by @AndreVto in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/793
* Upload achievements in real-time & custom achievement pop-up placements by @phobos665 in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/975
* Fix display of game playtime by @kiequoo in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/957
* Preserve settings on Steam logout by @xXJSONDeruloXx in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/903
* Winlator ludashi effects by @xXJSONDeruloXx in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/803
* Improve boot splash tip readability on dark displays by @nacl-dev in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/823
* Launch games in the correct workingDir, fix some cloud saves and no launches by @kiequoo in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/775
* Reduces boot time on external storage by @giovannygb in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/747
* Fix CursorWindow crash on large Steam libraries by @jeremybernstein in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/961
* add battery temperature metric to performance HUD by @xXJSONDeruloXx in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/991
* Nicer amazon images by @unbelievableflavour in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/1021
* Nicer images for in GOG detail screens by @unbelievableflavour in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/998
* Reduce buggy game calculation size (eg in RE4) by @jeremybernstein in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/929
* Adjust color scheme of the External Display Input (on Thor-type devices) by @MayRedBeWithYou in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/838


And many more:
* Add connector context to XConnectorEpoll logs by @xXJSONDeruloXx in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/869
* Fixed a bug with steam 2017 vc redists by @unbelievableflavour in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/886
* feat: performance hud customizability, adtl params by @xXJSONDeruloXx in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/877
* Fix SD card game detection after crash by @jeremybernstein in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/889
* Added "not logged in" splash screens by @unbelievableflavour in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/844
* Update manifest.json by @utkarshdalal in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/879
* Bugfix: Launch deps should be run in offline mode by @unbelievableflavour in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/898
* fix: allow downloads over VPN connections by @jeremybernstein in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/882
* Fix Steam WebSocket idle disconnects by @jeremybernstein in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/771
* Fix cold-launch intent race, add failure feedback by @jeremybernstein in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/884
* Fix dpad ordinality on dpad that allows opposing directions to be pressed simultaneously by @iobg in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/805
* fix(): update achievement background for better contrast by @phobos665 in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/836
* Moved image fetching to custom games by @unbelievableflavour in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/810
* fix(): silent hill 4 reg fixes by @phobos665 in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/926
* Fix carousel external mouse scrolling and dragging by @xXJSONDeruloXx in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/931
* Support old manifest download size format (Assassin's Creed 1 fix) by @unbelievableflavour in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/924
* Add Steam cloud saves browser shortcut by @xXJSONDeruloXx in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/840
* Feat/perf hud text outline by @xXJSONDeruloXx in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/915
* Added launch argument gamefixes by @unbelievableflavour in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/930
* Fix intent launch edge cases: two-tier snackbar, offline fallback by @jeremybernstein in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/912
* Fix "Resume" button staying after completed download/update by @jeremybernstein in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/923
* fix: show Steam sign-in in menu after logout by @jeremybernstein in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/790
* refactor: poison Toast and migrate to SnackbarManager by @jeremybernstein in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/851
* Skip symlinks in exe search by @unbelievableflavour in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/900
* fix: stabilize carousel cpu perf on idle by @xXJSONDeruloXx in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/968
* Fix expired license filtering without JSON string parsing by @jeremybernstein in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/985
* fix: prevent quick menu controller tab focus bounce by @xXJSONDeruloXx in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/976
* fix: preserve Steam session state on unexpected auth loss by @xXJSONDeruloXx in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/973
* fix: defer intent launches until GOG/Epic/Amazon service is ready by @jeremybernstein in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/987
* feat: allow disabling of cloud saves per game by @xXJSONDeruloXx in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/596
* fix: correct FEX_SMC_CHECKS env var to FEX_SMCCHECKS by @jeremybernstein in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/990
* Make images fill their container for library layout: hero by @unbelievableflavour in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/1003
* feat: add new keyboard QuickMenu navigation keys by @AndreVto in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/946

releases.name: v0.8.1
releases.body:
## What's Changed

- Added Steam achievement support!
- Correctly install prerequisites like VCRedist, PhysX, XNA, etc by @unbelievableflavour
- Add quick menu performance HUD overlay by @xXJSONDeruloXx in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/777
- Fix bug for Steam downloads where they get stuck on "Resume" in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/767
- Fix local saves being overwritten with cloud saves if connection lost during gameplay by @xXJSONDeruloXx in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/791
- Fixed infinite booting screens, black screens on boot and improved ability to download contents from our endpoints
- Fixes Edit Container crashes on older Android versions by @Catpotatos in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/753
- Fix external game launches while app is open & avoid pop-ups on external launch by @phobos665 in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/759
- Added container config export/import for all platforms by @unbelievableflavour in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/649
- Added per-game auto-pause/resume settings by @xXJSONDeruloXx in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/746
- Fixed VirGL in Glibc containers - Will enable Mali users to play 2D games in Glibc by @Catpotatos in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/763
- Fixed touchpad input by @AndreVto in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/725
- Added language-support for gen 1 GOG downloader by @unbelievableflavour in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/743
- Added language support for Epic (also shrinks game sizes) by @unbelievableflavour in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/754
- Replace Toast with Compose Snackbar system by @jeremybernstein in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/697
- Show games on internal and external storage at the same time by @jeremybernstein in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/781
- Allow searching for game titles with diacritics removed by @danbruno in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/712
- add fexcore 2603 by @phobos665 in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/723
- remove trim for passwords to fix trailing spaces in some user passwords by @phobos665 in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/736
- Fix: Closing FileInputStream and BufferedReader on listRunningWineProcesses by @giovannygb in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/756
- refactor: deduplicate network connectivity checks by @jeremybernstein in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/648
- Fix app crash in race condition in DownloadInfo ETA by snapshotting speed samples by @unbelievableflavour in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/755
- fix: resume immediately when opening keyboard in manual suspend mode by @xXJSONDeruloXx in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/779
- Translation into Russian by @Denis11212 in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/695

Bugfixes from v0.8.1-prerelease:
* fix: skip achievements generation when Steam not logged in by @jeremybernstein in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/835
* fix: allow intent launches for non-Steam and offline-mode games by @jeremybernstein in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/832
* Added app screen gradient so back button stays visible by @unbelievableflavour in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/843
* Fix/cpu affinity empty selection crash by @xXJSONDeruloXx in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/764
* fix: wait for exit sync before returning to launcher by @xXJSONDeruloXx in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/792
* fix(): Epic name mismatch between game and container ID + added logging for container deletion to catch orphans by @Catpotatos in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/760
* Fix crash on empty depot list (#729) by @jeremybernstein in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/772
* fix: update action shows Resume instead of download progress by @jeremybernstein in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/806
* Updated achievement uploading code to not skip achievements.json when… by @utkarshdalal in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/847
* Cleaned up UI on Portrait screens (on game page) and on small landsca… by @utkarshdalal in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/862
* Consolidated networking code to always attempt Google DNS, should pre… by @utkarshdalal in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/861
* Handle XServerView lifecycle across app sleep by @xXJSONDeruloXx in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/853
* fix: SD card games unrecoverable when external storage toggle off by @jeremybernstein in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/809

## New Contributors
* @Denis11212 made their first contribution in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/695
* @danbruno made their first contribution in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/712
* @Catpotatos made their first contribution in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/753
* @giovannygb made their first contribution in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/756

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/compare/v0.8.0...v0.8.1

releases.name: v0.8.0
releases.body:
## What's Changed
* [HOTFIX] Added back Reverse Landscape and setting allowed orientations
* Complete UI/UX overhaul by @ObfuscatedVoid , @phobos665, @xXJSONDeruloXx and me (in chronological order)
* Added Amazon games support by @xXJSONDeruloXx 
* Added dynamic sticks for touchscreen controller (left and right sticks will show up on touch) + a new touchscreen preset for strategy games by @Nightwalker743 in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/599
* Improve Cloud Saves for Epic Games by @phobos665 in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/589
* Fixed external mouse by @jeremybernstein and @AndreVto 
* Fix rumble on devices such as Ayn Odin 2 by @kiequoo in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/538
* Various compatibility and bugfixes for GOG by @unbelievableflavour
* Correctly download audio after changing language for Steam games by @joshuatam in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/507
* Correctly download audio after changing language for GOG games by @unbelievableflavour in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/592
* Several bugfixes, including correctly applying FEX preset, crashes with large logs and custom exes, fixing fetching of SteamGridDB images, fixing affinity not being applied, downloading missing proton on first install and more by @jeremybernstein 
* Fixed L2 and R2 being registered as buttons instead of axes
* Pause games on bringing up in-game menu by @xXJSONDeruloXx 
* Added better support for pocket taco/8bitdo flippad type portrait controllers in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/541
* Add new manifest attribute to ensure app is classified as a game - will help with Samsung Game Launcher by @Unnvaldr in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/709
* Added steam offline mode for games like N++
* Update pulseaudio component for fixing loss of audio when pausing by @joshuatam in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/534
* Fixed second-screen keyboard staying visible for AYN Thor by @xXJSONDeruloXx 
* fix(l10n): Refine Spanish translations and fix a small UI bug by @zeedif in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/511
* Add Korean (ko) locale support by @TeamConceptKR in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/512
* Feedback for non-steam games now supported by @phobos665 in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/577
* add toggle for auto-apply known config by @jeremybernstein in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/586
* use header image instead of capsule image if missing for steam games by @silentrald in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/555
* Hotfix for Polish Language to be included in build.gradle.kts by @Petexy in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/529

## New Contributors
* @TeamConceptKR made their first contribution in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/512
* @xXJSONDeruloXx made their first contribution in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/531

* @Nightwalker743 made their first contribution in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/599
* @AndreVto made their first contribution in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/664
* @Unnvaldr made their first contribution in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/709

* Working Game Shortcuts for all game sources by @phobos665 in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/423

## What's Changed
* Epic library support by @phobos665

releases.body:
## What's Changed
* Added ability to add custom games by @maaggel
* Show compatibility of games within GameNative, allow applying of known configs for games
* Cleaned up and fixed download issues, estimated time is far more accurate and download UI is better
* Added new wrapper driver, significantly improve compatibility for non-Adreno devices, reduce glitches, increase performance in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/296
* Added Proton 10 as downloadable content, added ability to include custom wine and proton wcps by @phobos665 
* Brought in some new Turnip Drivers and Box64 by @phobos665 in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/294
* Added pull down to refresh gamelist by @maaggel in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/308
* Added beta support for Denuvo
* Added Brazilian Portuguese, Danish and Simplified Chinese translations by @rodrigosiviero, @maaggel, @darkcl, @phobos665 
* Fixed crashes when using external storage external storage by @phobos665 in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/317
* Correctly pause and resume games when app paused or screen turned off by @Phr3d13  in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/322

releases.name: v0.5.2
releases.body:
## What's Changed
* Reduced heat while downloading, made downloads faster, fixed games that would not download previously by integrating JavaSteam 1.8.0. Thanks @LossyDragon!
* Overhauled DRM to make more games work (eg Halo MCC)
* Reduced crashes, improved app responsiveness generally
* Added automatic updates in-app, no more needing to go to GitHub
* Use avg frame rate and session length for compatibility list
* Fixed games that have both 32-bit and 64-bit depots by @jeremybernstein in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/206
* Add com.oculus.vrshellsupports_free_resizing to allow window resizing on Oculus Quest headsets by @goopsie in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/221
* Added option to go into offline mode manually in app
* Allow downloads over ethernet
* Small fix for launching via Steam client
* Initial support for multiple languages by @phobos665 in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/249
* dxvk-2.7.1 added by @phobos665 in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/261
* FEXCore-2511 added by @phobos665 in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/253
* game title list borders by @phobos665 in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/247
* Added functionality to hide topbar when not in game by @Maaggel in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/242

## New Contributors
* @goopsie made their first contribution in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/221
* @jeremybernstein made their first contribution in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/206
* @phobos665 made their first contribution in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/249

* improved error handling while downloading

releases.name: v0.5.2-prerelease2
releases.body:
## What's Changed
* Added option to go into offline mode manually
* Store session length and avg frame rate when submitting feedback
* Allow downloads over ethernet
* Fix for launch with steam client
* Reduced heat while downloading, made downloads faster, fixed games that would not download previously by integrating JavaSteam 1.8.0. Thanks @LossyDragon!
* Migrate all UI Strings to String Templates to enable Localisation Work by @phobos665 in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/249
* Overhauled DRM to make more games work (eg Halo MCC)
* dxvk-2.7.1 added by @phobos665 in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/261
* FEXCore-2511 added by @phobos665 in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/253
* Show game name in grid/hero view by @phobos665 in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/247
* Added functionality to hide topbar when not in game by @Maaggel in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/242

## New Contributors
* @phobos665 made their first contribution in https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/249

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/compare/v0.5.2-prerelease1...v0.5.2-prerelease2

releases.name: v0.5.2-prerelease1
releases.body:

* Fixed huge bug where DXVK was not installing for games unless Edit Container was clicked, and configs saved

* Fixed default configs not saving for driver under wrapper in bionic

* Added ability to remove env vars without them coming back every time

releases.name: Winlator 10.2 (Revision 2 Beta) Glibc unstable - Early Access
releases.body:
[![logo](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/bd81f1a1-2391-4c39-abbd-a42b1f5ef65f)](https://github.com/winebox64/winlator/releases)

# Released for personal use ### Winlator 10.2 (beta) - Now smaller, lighter, and faster! Save storage with optimized app packaged in 130MB and installed in just 1.3GB. - Updated latest DXVK Async (v2.6.1), and **DXVK-II ULL (v2.5.3.94)** focus on ultra low latency ~8ms at 60 FPS, ~4ms at 120 FPS, with slightly lower fps (small performance impact), Improves input responsiveness on NFS: Most Wanted 2012. ![lv_0_20250323083625](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a0aab4ec-0637-4cd2-a171-8ad5a76d91cb) **Definition:** *What is ULL? Ultra-low latency refers to an extremely short delay or lag between a user's input and the system's response. It's measured in milliseconds (ms) or even nanoseconds (ns).* | *Input Latency* | *Gaming Experience* | *Suitability* | | --- | --- | --- | | 0-30 ms | Excellent | Competitive Gaming | | 30-50 ms | Good | Casual Gaming | | 50-100 ms | Fair | Non-Competitive Play | | 100-200 ms | Poor | Not Recommended | | 200+ ms | Unacceptable | Unplayable | - Updated DXVK HUD scale and opacity options have been added. - Updated 2 new configuration presets are available: `optmx11` for improved graphics and `perfx11` for better performance, the choice is yours. - ZINK_DEBUG has been updated with additional options - Zink Descriptors has been updated with additional options. - Updated components now fixed. - The loading is now way smoother and loads faster! - Updated Screen size resolutions and wider aspect ratio. - Updated FrameRate options - Updated environment variables are now activated by default. ### Winlator 10.0 (beta) - Added missing MPEG-1 gstreamer plugin [#269](https://github.com/brunodev85/winlator/issues/269) - Added workaround for Dino Crisis 1/2 video issues - Updated Box64 (v0.3.4 release).~ - Added native GLIBC for better I/O performance - Minor improvements to Vortek - Improved internal Registry Editor (Speeds up container startup) - Added option to open Android browser from Wine - Added option to use Android clipboard on Wine - Added option to capture pointer on external mouse - Added some network stuff on [Wine](https://github.com/brunodev85/wine-9.2-custom/commit/3437f8ee1617ba97550c96fb2dc4e210420aae93) (Fixes some games startup) - Implemented direct rendering on XServer and [Vulkan WSI X11](https://github.com/brunodev85/mesa3d-custom/blob/main/turnip-25.0.0/src/vulkan/wsi/wsi_common_x11.c) (Turnip). ![animation](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/937e1f8a-2b21-4e26-95a7-37020400bc60) ---- ### ⚠️ **Results can vary based on the device used, environmental conditions, and other factors, such as simultaneous processing, and different processor capabilities.** **If you experience errors or crashes, try changing DXVK, Graphics driver or Box64.*

releases.name: Winlator 9.1.1 Early Access
releases.body:
[![logo](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/bd81f1a1-2391-4c39-abbd-a42b1f5ef65f)](https://github.com/winebox64/winlator/releases)

## (Early Access) - Updated WINE (Custom) improved compatibility. - Updated Box64 0.3.3 (Custom) 📥 downloadable. - Updated Turnip Devel version 📥 downloadable. - Updated DXVK Async version 📥 downloadable. - Updated Vortek (Experimental) is for processors that are incompatible with the Turnip driver, such as Snapdragon 8 Elite, 860, 735, 7 Gen 3, 7s Gen 3, 7s Gen 2, 6 Gen 3, 6 Gen 1. - Updated Vulkan WSI environment. - Updated DXVK_HUD opacity & scale variable. - Updated ZINK_DEBUG added more options `fast_texture` `fast_path` `fast_shader` etc. - Updated TU_DEBUG added more options `render_fast` `memory_align` `memory_leak` etc. - Updated FSync for smoother. - Updated Zink Descriptors `db` Descriptor Buffer option to Reduced descriptors update overhead. - Updated WINE GST options. - Reduced system temperature and improved stability. ---- **How to download and change the Box64, DXVK, Turnip version, e.g:**

### ⚠️ **Results can vary based on the device used, environmental conditions, and other factors, such as simultaneous processing, and different processor capabilities.** **If you experience errors or crashes, try changing DXVK or Graphics driver.*

releases.name: Winlator 8.5.5
releases.body:
![logo](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/bd81f1a1-2391-4c39-abbd-a42b1f5ef65f)

# (Early Access) - Updated Wine Environment. - Updated Dynarec Forward & Fast Rounding. - Updated Mesa Turnip drivers 2.5.0.0-xx. - Updated DXVK 2.5.1.2-xx-xxx. - Updated HDR supported. - Updated Async environment variable. - Updated State cache environment variable. - Updated Caches pipeline state, which can reduce stuttering. - Updated GPL can be used together with state cache that is not possible on upstream, state cache should work properly with async and gpl if using Async cache option. - Updated Screen Size Aspect Ratio. - Updated Device Memory Limit, fixed crash. - Updated FrameRate variable. - Updated opcodes, workarounds for A6xx. - Fixed audio in some application. - Reduce RAM usage up to ~11.5% and performance optimization. ### ⚠️ NOTE !!: **Results can vary based on the device used, environmental conditions, and other factors, such as simultaneous processing, and different processor capabilities.** **Note: If you experience crashes, try changing another Graphics driver that suits your device.*

releases.name: Turnip A8XX v24
releases.body:
A8XX Turnip Vulkan V24  
Built from: https://github.com/whitebelyash/mesa-tu8. Buh buh buh

I didn't test this this much. Contact me on FEX (#android-wastelands) or EmuCore discord if there are any issues.

>[!WARNING]
> Please don't report issues to Mesa, PLEASE
          
Supported GPUs:
* Adreno 8XX Family: 840, 830, 829, 825 (please test this one, it might be broken), 810
* Adreno 7XX Family: any GPU supported by the upstream Turnip + 710/720 from Termux patches
* Adreno 6XX Family: any GPU supported by the upstream Turnip

GPUs other than 830/840 can have reduced performance and/or more glitches. Can't do anything about that, sorry!  
Adreno 810 is additionally very slow, I'm not sure if you even want to use Turnip there

Files:
* gen8 - main branch (upstream MR + hacks applied)

Notes:
* Disable DXVK_HUD if you see weird black artifacts
* Apparently there are swizzling glitches in Eden & maybe other emulators, try increasing render scale(?)
* Some games may require AMD/NVIDIA GPU to run and refuse to start on Qualcomm or block some features. I've included a patch to spoof as Steam Deck - use `TU_DEBUG=deck_emu`
* GMEM rendering produces glitchy picture on A830 (write page faults), disable it with `TU_DEBUG=sysmem`. It's pretty much useless for PC emulation anyway.

What's new:
* Upstream rebase (lots of changes from there)
* All A8XX GPU variants do not have "Turnip" prefix anymore (upstream)
* Better configs for A829 and A810 (based on upstream non-merged MRs, still better than my untested blindly added config entries)

flushall patch is included. KGSL timeline MR is not merged here due to Draft status.

releases.name: Turnip A8XX vdont_use
releases.body:
A8XX Turnip Vulkan Vdont_use  
Built from: https://github.com/whitebelyash/mesa-tu8

MojoLauncher build test (because dogshit CI produces completely broken builds for whatever reasons, I'm tired)

releases.name: Mesa Turnip v26.1.0-git_3
releases.body:
Mesa Turnip V26.1.0-git_3  
Built from: https://gitlab.freedesktop.org/mesa/mesa

>[!WARNING]
> GPUs other than A830/A840 may work unstable!

This is a build of current upstream Mesa with my GPU enablement hacks applied

Changes:
- Import upstream as usual
- Decrease reg_size on 830 to 96 - should fix Cyberpunk 2077 crashes. See the commit info.
- Integrate [unmerged](https://gitlab.freedesktop.org/mesa/mesa/-/merge_requests/39751) KGSL timeline sync MR - works good enough now. This is a proper replacement for old cringy vibecoded timeline sync emulation code I removed few releases ago. 
- Remove gmem cache offset completely as it breaks on lower-end A8XX GPUs.
- due to that, change cache sizes for A825/A810. GMEM should work on A825 now.
- Integrate zdobersek's patch fixing (workaround'ing) FLUSHALL. There's one build now as flushall is disabled by default and fixed.

>[!WARNING]

releases.name: Mesa Turnip v26.1.0-git
releases.body:
Mesa Turnip V26.1.0-git  
Built from: https://gitlab.freedesktop.org/mesa/mesa

>[!WARNING]
> GPUs other than A840 may work unstable!

This is a build of current upstream Mesa with my GPU enablement hacks applied

Changes compared to v23:
* Upstream Mesa
* Now doing new builds by applying my patches up on upstream Mesa-git branch instead of cloning my repo (I have updated it as well)
* Removed vibecoded timeline sync emulation shit and other unneeded hacks (read the commit message before blaming me for using AI AI AI)
* Started doing flushall & noflushall builds - flushall is still enabled for A8XX for various reasons
* Various changes in freedreno/tu from Mesa (still no proper A830 config)
* Disabled thin LTO and LTO in general (apparently causes weird bugs in RADV, might also apply for Turnip)
* Maybe something else...

Didn't test this release on my device, but should work well.  

I've also seen interesting Turnip builds with some "cool" fixes, but neither me interested in looking into patches (in sense of asking for sources or searching the repo) nor the author wants to share `git format-patch` of their fork (FWIW I shared all my patches in specific Telegram group), so I care less.

releases.name: Turnip A8XX v23
releases.body:
A8XX Turnip Vulkan V23  
Built from: https://github.com/whitebelyash/mesa-tu8

>[!WARNING]
> This release is still Beta, especially for devices other than Adreno 840. PLEASE, don't report any issues to Mesa
> Original merge request is Draft again, so it's not ready
          
Supports following GPUs:
- Adreno 810 (tested, working, less stable than all other GPUs) - sysmem only
- Adreno 825 (tested, working with minor glitches compared to A830) - GMEM/sysmem
- Adreno 830 (tested, working) - sysmem-only - gmem causes GPU hangs
- Adreno 840 (tested, working) - GMEM/sysmem
Following GPUs were added but no proper testing was done:
- Adreno 829 (no testers were found)

GPUs other than 830/840 can have reduced performance and/or more glitches. Can't do anything about that, sorry!  
Adreno 810 is additionally very slow, I'm not sure if you even want to use Turnip there

Files:
* gen8 - main branch (upstream MR + hacks applied)

Notes:
* Disable DXVK_HUD if you see weird black artifacts
* Apparently there are swizzling glitches in Eden & maybe other emulators, try increasing render scale(?)
* Some games may require AMD/NVIDIA GPU to run and refuse to start on Qualcomm or block some features. I've included a patch to spoof as Steam Deck - use `TU_DEBUG=deck_emu`
* If too unstable - activate nolrz: `TU_DEBUG=nolrz`.

* Apparently there are swizzling glitches in Eden & maybe other emulators, try increasing render scale(?)
* Some games may require AMD/NVIDIA GPU to run and refuse to start on Qualcomm or block some features. I've included a patch to spoof as Steam Deck - use `TU_DEBUG=deck_emu`
* If too unstable - activate nolrz: `TU_DEBUG=nolrz` or flushall: `TU_DEBUG=flushall`

Changes:
- turnip: Implement VK_EXT_device_memory_report (upstream)
- turnip: Re-enable lrz (upstream)
- freedreno: Force single wavesize if double threadsize is unsupported (upstream)
- rebase (downstream)
- fix A810 rendering TestD3D cube(?) (downstream)
- make A829 render at least something (downstream)

releases.name: Turnip A8XX v20
releases.body:
A8XX Turnip Vulkan V20  
Built from: https://github.com/whitebelyash/mesa-tu8

>[!WARNING]
> This release is still Beta, especially for devices other than Adreno 840. PLEASE, don't report any issues to Mesa
> Original merge request is Draft again, so it's not ready

Supports following GPUs:
- Adreno 810 (tested, working at least in TestD3D) - might be sysmem-only, didn't test gmem
- Adreno 825 (tested, working with minor glitches compared to A830) - GMEM/sysmem
- Adreno 830 (tested, working) - sysmem-only - gmem causes GPU hangs
- Adreno 840 (tested, working) - GMEM/sysmem

Following GPUs were added but no proper testing was done:  
- Adreno 829 (no testers were found). It works fine with drm-shim, so maybe same for real devices?

GPUs other than 830/840 can have reduced performance and/or more glitches. Can't do anything about that, sorry!  
Additionally A810 cache size is awfully small, so don't expect magic performance on Turnip.

Files:
* gen8 - main branch (upstream MR + hacks applied)

Notes:
* Disable DXVK_HUD if you see weird black artifacts
* Apparently there are swizzling glitches in Eden & maybe other emulators, try increasing render scale(?)
* Some games may require AMD/NVIDIA GPU to run and refuse to start on Qualcomm or block some features. I've included a patch to spoof as Steam Deck - use `TU_DEBUG=deck_emu`
* If too unstable - activate nolrz: `TU_DEBUG=nolrz`.

Changes:
* Import upstream changes
* Add proper initial support for Adreno 810. It's now rendering a cube!  
* Add support for A710/A720. These were full copies of A730 with only different ids in termux mesa patches.

The issue with A810 is its very small gmem cache (576KB). This caused an integer underflow, because nobody thought of A810 and its so-small cache, breaking rendering completely as invalid value was written onto `RB_CCU_CACHE_CNTL` register. I have discovered this with a debug build of Mesa, it was hitting an assert even with drm-shim due to this underflow.

releases.name: Turnip A8XX vgralloc-exp
releases.body:
A8XX Turnip Vulkan Vgralloc-exp (V14)

Supports following GPUs:
- Adreno 825 (tested, working with minor glitches compared to A830) - GMEM/sysmem
- Adreno 830 (tested, working) - sysmem-only - gmem causes GPU hangs
- Adreno 840 (tested, working) - GMEM/sysmem
Following GPUs were added but no proper testing was done:
- Adreno 810 (had blackscreen as I assumed it's duo-slice, now the rendering should work, but I'm not sure)
- Adreno 829 (no testers were found)

GPUs other than 830/840 can have reduced performance and/or more glitches. Can't do anything about that, sorry!

Files:
* gen8-hacks - main branch (upstream MR + hacks applied)
* ~~gen8-yuck - branch with some commits reverted to gain back perf in RDR2. Probably useless at this point~~

Settings:
* Disable DXVK_HUD
* ~~Enable swapchain blit path to workaround corrupted image on Android: `WRAPPER_BLIT=1` Not required on GameHub~~
* ~~Activate sysmem on A830 - everything will hang/crash/etc without: `TU_DEBUG=sysmem`. Not required on A840/A825~~
Now disabled automatically
* If too unstable - activate nolrz: `TU_DEBUG=nolrz`.

Changes:
* Added a workaround to fix u_gralloc skipping setting drm format modifier type - all weird pixel artifacts without WRAPPER_BLIT=1 should be gone now!

More info:
Fallback gralloc implementation in Mesa has this check to set proper drm format modifier for ubwc/nonubwc swapchain images (ubwc is a Qualcomm compression):
```c
   if (hnd->handle->numInts >= 2 && hnd->handle->data[hnd->handle->numFds] == gmsm) {
      /* This UBWC flag was introduced in a5xx. */
      bool ubwc = hnd->handle->data[hnd->handle->numFds + 1] & 0x08000000;
      out->modifier = ubwc ? DRM_FORMAT_MOD_QCOM_COMPRESSED : DRM_FORMAT_MOD_LINEAR;
   }
```
Looks like a8xx changed something here thus this condition fails and drm format isn't being set at all. This was the reason of garbled output in most native Vulkan apps.  
Removing the check is enough to overcome this issue.

releases.name: Turnip A8XX v13.1
releases.body:
A8XX Turnip Vulkan V13.1

Supports following GPUs:
- Adreno 825 (tested, working with minor glitches compared to A830) - GMEM/sysmem
- Adreno 830 (tested, working) - sysmem-only - gmem causes GPU hangs
- Adreno 840 (tested, working) - GMEM/sysmem
Following GPUs were added but no proper testing was done:
- Adreno 810 (had blackscreen as I assumed it's duo-slice, now the rendering should work, but I'm not sure)
- Adreno 829 (no testers were found)

GPUs other than 830/840 can have reduced performance and/or more glitches. Can't do anything about that, sorry!

Files:
* gen8-hacks - main branch (upstream MR + hacks applied)
~~* gen8-yuck - branch with some commits reverted to gain back perf in RDR2. Probably useless at this point~~

Settings:
* Disable DXVK_HUD
* Enable swapchain blit path to workaround corrupted image on Android: `WRAPPER_BLIT=1` Not required on GameHub
* ~~Activate sysmem on A830 - everything will hang/crash/etc without: `TU_DEBUG=sysmem`. Not required on A840/A825~~
Now disabled automatically
* If too unstable - activate nolrz: `TU_DEBUG=nolrz`.

Changes:
* Revert "WIP: freedreno: use gen2 config for A825"

releases.name: Turnip A8XX v13
releases.body:
A8XX Turnip Vulkan V13

Supports following GPUs:
- Adreno 825 (tested, working with minor glitches compared to A830) - GMEM/sysmem
- Adreno 830 (tested, working) - sysmem-only - gmem causes GPU hangs
- Adreno 840 (tested, working) - GMEM/sysmem

Following GPUs were added but no proper testing was done:
- Adreno 810 (had blackscreen as I assumed it's duo-slice, now the rendering should work, but I'm not sure)
- Adreno 829 (no testers were found)

GPUs other than 830/840 can have reduced performance and/or more glitches. Can't do anything about that, sorry!

Files:
* gen8-hacks - main branch (upstream MR + hacks applied)
~~* gen8-yuck - branch with some commits reverted to gain back perf in RDR2. Probably useless at this point~~

Settings:
* Disable DXVK_HUD
* Enable swapchain blit path to workaround corrupted image on Android: `WRAPPER_BLIT=1` Not required on GameHub
* ~~Activate sysmem on A830 - everything will hang/crash/etc without: `TU_DEBUG=sysmem`. Not required on A840/A825~~
Now disabled automatically
* If too unstable - activate nolrz: `TU_DEBUG=nolrz`.

Changes:
* Disabled GMEM on A830/A810 - these GPUs have broken GMEM configuration causing GPU hangs in the default usage (i.e. without sysmem debug variable). This change allows the driver to work on A830/A810 without mentioned variable.
* Stopped building yuck branch builds - I'm yet to see real improvement of reverting useful commits.

releases.name: Turnip A8XX v12.1
releases.body:
Turnip Vulkan driver library Release V12.1

Files:
* gen8-hacks - main branch (upstream MR + hacks applied)
* gen8-yuck - branch with some commits reverted to gain back perf in RDR2. Probably useless at this point

Settings:
* Disable DXVK_HUD
* Enable swapchain blit path to workaround corrupted image on Android: `WRAPPER_BLIT=1`
* **Activate sysmem on A830 - everything will hang/crash/etc without**: `TU_DEBUG=sysmem`. Not required on A840/A825
* If too unstable - activate nolrz: `TU_DEBUG=nolrz` or `TU_DEBUG=sysmem,nolrz` if A830.

releases.name: Turnip A8XX v12
releases.body:
Turnip Vulkan driver library Release V12

Files:
* gen8-hacks - main branch (upstream MR + hacks applied)
* gen8-yuck - branch with some commits reverted to gain back perf in RDR2. Probably useless at this point

Settings:
* Disable DXVK_HUD
* Enable swapchain blit path to workaround corrupted image on Android: `WRAPPER_BLIT=1`
* **Activate sysmem on A830 - everything will hang/crash/etc without**: `TU_DEBUG=sysmem`. Not required on A840/A825.
*this is because A830 config has invalid gmem attribute values - these will be fixed soon*
* If too unstable - activate nolrz: `TU_DEBUG=nolrz` or `TU_DEBUG=sysmem,nolrz` if A830.

Changes:
* The upstream MR is now ready to merge! (the "Draft" status is no more)
* More upstream commits

releases.name: EGW 3.0.2 BASED CACHE MOD
releases.body:
### EGW 3.0.2.2 UPDATE (1/13/24)

THIS CACHE IS VERY OLD AND ONLY SUITABLE FOR OLD GAMES... GAMES MAY NOT RUN PROPERLY.

APK CHANGES:
- Added Inject Document Provider (MT Manager)
- Removed Languages Option in Container Properties
- Removed GPU Option in Container Properties
- Removed All language string values on the APK.
- Removed 3 Others Option in the Container Properties
- Changed the default resolution to 800x600
- Changed the default language to English
- Changed the default renderer to VirGL
- Changed the GALLIUM_HUD variables
- Added Some Container Icons
- Added Change Location of Drives from EDPatch
- Added Custom Controls and New VirGL UI

CACHE CHANGES:
- PulseAudio libraries has been removed
- Removed "sbin, sys, run, root, proc, cron, rsyslog, log, cache" and many other files/libs
- Removed many __useless__ libraries (40MB reduced)
- Added Old 7Zip v19.00 and installed by default
- Added Adobe Flash Player Standalone for .swf games
- Added IrfanView v4.6.0 as default
- Changed Compressor from WinRAR to 7Zip
- Added 50 WineD3D DLLs to choose
- Added Many DSOAL + OpenAL alternatives
- Added Mesa VirGL v4 renderers
- Added Benchmarking Tools
- Added PhysX Legacy and AGEIA PhysX fix for old games
- Fixed file icons from Hugo
- Pre-Installed Input Bridge 0.1.9.9

SYSTEM32 CHANGES:
- Added DirectX & Codecs missing dlls
- CoreFonts will be installed by default
- Added DirectShow, DirectPlay dlls and some fixes
- Added bink32.dll in system32 version 1.99a
- Added Visual C .NET dlls 2002/2003
- Added Miles Sound System dll in system32
- Added WinG/GDI plus dlls for old games
- Added d3dto8 wrapper
MORE CHANGES ARE UNLISTED.

releases.name: [OLD] EGW 3.0.2 BASED CACHE

### [OLD] EGW 3.0.2 BASED CACHE 

This is from October of 2022.

- Fixed Missing Mouse Icon

Updates:

- Added PhysX, WinImage, VB-Runtime, DxWnd, DgVoodoo2
- Fix Some Missing DLL's
- Removed Software Rendering
- Built-In OBB
- Added 3dfX Games Compatability

THIS IS GOOD FOR VERY OLD 3D GAMES AND 2D GAMES ONLY USES ONLY VIRGL GOOD FOR LOW-END-DEVICES fullspeed is not guaranteed

releases.name: v1.3.2
releases.body:
Fixes:
- Notification action "Exit" not terminating service when not in focus. - @LossyDragon @oxters168
- Proper fix for library padding crash from 1.3.1 hotfix. - @LossyDragon
- Left pane in a 2-Pane layout should fill the width properly (Library and Friends list) - @LossyDragon
- Hide keyboard when pressing search/enter button. - @LossyDragon
- Fix family shared games missing from library list @LossyDragon @oxters168

Additions:
- New app icon/logo by @Prowi3 Huge THANK YOU for this awesome icon!
- Apply new icons for Pluvia. - @LossyDragon @oxters168
- Expose some debug options in Settings. - @LossyDragon
- Friends list now shows game names. - @LossyDragon
- "Family" filter item for library list - @LossyDragon

Dependency Updates: 
- JavaSteam 1.6.1 with Family Sharing support and misc fixes.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/oxters168/Pluvia/compare/v1.3.1...v1.3.2

releases.name: v1.3.1
releases.body:
# Hotfix
Fix of crash for top padding must be non-negative

releases.name: v1.3.0
releases.body:
Additions: 
- [Preference] Day, Night, Auto theme.
- [Preference] Theme color palette.
- [Preference] Which screen to launch on. (Library, Downloads, or Friends).
- Chatting with friends, with message history, emoticon and sticker support. 
- Friend profiles, with basic friend management.
- Option to view external web links in app or externally with a browser of choice.
- Game app screen shows basic information.
- More library filer options, which are retained. 

Fixes: 
- Profile dialog works better for long names now.
- Crash when null messages are logged. 
- Crashes when a failed sign in occurred, such as invalid credentials. 
- Correct display of install/run button and download progress for apps. Thanks @Producdevity
- Add smooth transitions for download state. Thanks @Producdevity
- Friends list groups able to be collapsible now.
- Library scroll position should remember when navigating away.
- Library list optimization.
- Soft resize issue with on-screen keyboard is opened/closed.
- Rotating the screen closes some dialogs.
- Dialog list scrolling on small screen height. Thanks @Producdevity
- Themed app icon being oversized.
- Game app screen image loading shifting.

Changes: 
- Connection to steam now uses the Web Socket protocol.
- Login errors are shown in a snackbar.
- Various theme unification for light and dark mode.
- Various Compose tweaks.
- Access tokens now encrypted.
- Removed automatic app backup.
- Actively filter out Spacewar (game).
- Library filter options now is a bottom sheet modal.

Misc: 
- Add Github Actions for snapshot and validation builds.
- Update readme.
- Dependency updates. Now using JavaSteam 1.6.0.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/oxters168/Pluvia/compare/v1.2.0...v1.3.0

releases.name: v1.2.0
releases.body:
## What's Changed

- Readme updates.
- Add library search functionality.
- Add container settings
    - Add per app container settings.
    - Add default app container settings (Settings).
    - Add Box64 presets (Settings). 
- Change how the crash log report is organized.
- Allow the login screen to scroll when keyboard takes up too much of the screen when in landscape
- Fix the service from being stopped and started in login screen when device is rotated.
- Fix retry button in login screen when QR login times out.
- Fix infinite loading when signing out of Pluvia.
- Show which Fab filter is applied in Library list.
- JavaSteam snapshot updates.
- Various internal refactoring.


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/oxters168/Pluvia/compare/v1.1.0...v1.2.0

releases.name: v1.1.0
releases.body:
## What's Changed
* Better logging support by @LossyDragon in https://github.com/oxters168/Pluvia/pull/22
* Update README.md by @LossyDragon in https://github.com/oxters168/Pluvia/pull/23
* Add exit button to service notification. by @LossyDragon in https://github.com/oxters168/Pluvia/pull/24
* Custom crash handler by @LossyDragon in https://github.com/oxters168/Pluvia/pull/25
* Fixed issue during 2FA login by @LossyDragon in https://github.com/Longi94/JavaSteam/pull/314
* Changed version name to use semantic versioning by @oxters168 in https://github.com/oxters168/Pluvia/pull/33


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/oxters168/Pluvia/compare/v1.0...v1.1.0

releases.name: CMOD_Benchmark
releases.body:
Latest changes from the developer.
Fixed graphical response of pressing the button by coffincolors.
The controller will _not_ work with this version.
Set box64-0.4.1-fix, wowbox64-0.4.1-fix, fexcore-2601 and turnip-26.0.0 by StevenMXZ as default.
Advanced box64 profile settings.
Use apk's at your own risk.

_Assets:_

- **CMOD_Genshin:**
 Can activate hidden features like frame interpolation on Realme devices.
- **CMOD_AnTuTu:**
 Can maximize clocks of all single cores of the CPU part on certain devices.
- **CMOD_3DMark:**
 Can maximize GPU utilization on certain devices. (needs further testing).
